title: Incidence and prevalence of extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis
subtitle: The estimated number of people living with [extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis](#dod:drug-resistant-tuberculosis) (prevalence) versus new cases each year (incidence).
caption:
Incidence and prevalence of extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis, World

The estimated number of people living with extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis (prevalence) versus new cases each year (incidence).

Between 1990 and 2023, the global count of people living with extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis and the number of new cases each year both show sustained upward movement. The vertical axis measures the number of people, ranging from 0 to 35,000, while the horizontal axis spans years from 1990 to 2023. Two lines trace the data: one for prevalence, which records the total number of people living with the condition, and one for incidence, which records new cases in a given year. In 1990, both lines start near the bottom of the scale, close to zero. By 2000, prevalence reaches roughly 12,000 and incidence sits around 9,000. The gap between the two lines widens through the 2000s; by 2010, prevalence climbs to approximately 28,000 while incidence levels off near 21,000. From 2010 to 2015, the prevalence line continues to rise steadily, reaching about 29,000, while the incidence line shows a flatter trajectory, hovering just above 22,000. After 2015, both lines resume a clearer upward slope. By 2023, the prevalence line ends near 33,000, the highest point on the chart, while the incidence line finishes around 24,000. Throughout the entire period, the prevalence line remains above the incidence line, and the distance between them grows larger over time. The visible pattern shows that the total number of people living with extensively drug-resistant tuberculosis has consistently exceeded the number of new cases each year, with both metrics increasing over the 33-year span.
